meta_title: For Students: user onboarding to Solve no version control (Beginner Comparison)
meta_description: Learn how to user onboarding and overcome no version control. A Beginner-level Comparison using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Students: User Onboarding to Solve No Version Control
content.seo_description: Learn how to implement user onboarding to overcome no version control. A Beginner-level guide using Antigravity and Claude Code for your first projects.
content.answer_block: Without version control, your code is a house of cards. By implementing a structured user onboarding flow using Antigravity and Claude Code, you establish a reproducible environment. This forces you to modularize your logic, making state management easier and turning chaotic scripts into professional, version-ready software architecture.
content.intro:
You’ve spent days crafting your first feature, only to overwrite a working function with a broken experiment. The sinking feeling of 'no version control' is a rite of passage for every developer, but it’s a bottleneck that kills your productivity and scares away recruiters. You aren't just losing code; you're losing the ability to track your own growth.

But there is a better way. By treating your user onboarding flow as the foundation of your application, you force yourself to build in isolated, testable modules. Using modern AI-assisted tools, you can transform your workflow from 'hope-based development' to a robust, professional system that mirrors how senior engineers ship production software.

steps.title: Define the Onboarding State Machine
steps.description: Before writing code, map out the user journey. This prevents scope creep and ensures your backend handles progress tracking without relying on a single, messy file.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a state machine schema for a 3-step onboarding flow. Include transitions for 'welcome', 'profile_setup', and 'dashboard_access' to ensure state consistency.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --prompt 'Generate a JSON schema for a 3-step user onboarding flow with state validation.'

steps.title: Modularize the Welcome Component
steps.description: Break your onboarding UI into isolated components. This allows you to test individual views without crashing the entire app.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Refactor the onboarding UI into modular React components. Ensure each component accepts props for user progress to enable easy debugging.
steps.claude_code_command: claude edit --file src/onboarding/Welcome.tsx --patch 'Refactor to functional component with props.'

steps.title: Implement Progress Persistence
steps.description: Even without Git, you can use local storage to simulate versioned state. This ensures a user doesn't lose their progress if the browser refreshes.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a utility hook to persist onboarding progress in localStorage with validation logic to prevent data corruption.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --prompt 'Create a useOnboarding hook that saves state to localStorage with a version check.'

steps.title: Add Defensive Validation
steps.description: Add checks to ensure that if a user skips a step, the system redirects them back to the last known 'good' state.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a validation middleware that checks the onboarding state before rendering the dashboard, redirecting users to the last incomplete step.
steps.claude_code_command: claude edit --file src/routes/ProtectedRoute.tsx --patch 'Add onboarding state guard clause.'

steps.title: Automate Final Deployment Prep
steps.description: Use AI to package your project into a clean structure, preparing it for a transition to Git version control once you are ready.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze the current project structure and generate a directory manifest that organizes the code into clean, logical folders.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --prompt 'Review project structure and suggest a clean folder hierarchy for better maintainability.'

pro_tips: Treat every onboarding screen as a 'save point'—if you can't undo it, build a 'reset' button that clears state.
Use console.table() to debug your onboarding state object in real-time; it's cleaner than standard logs.
Always build your onboarding flow with a 'skip' option to prevent user frustration during testing.
Implement 'feature flags' in your local config; this allows you to toggle new UI experiments without deleting old code.
Document your logic in a README.md file—when you eventually add Git, this will be your first commit message.
content.next_steps: Once your onboarding flow is stable, initialize a Git repository immediately using 'git init'. Move your modularized files into the new structure and create your first commit. This is the moment you transition from a coder to a software engineer.
content.conversion_block: You now have a system that is modular, testable, and ready for version control. Don't let your code sit in a void—take the next step in your professional journey. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
